data.title: Oracle Sector Faces Intensified Divergence Amid Governance Disputes and Market Shifts
data.description: The oracle sector is experiencing increased divergence as various platforms face distinct challenges and opportunities. According to PANews, UMA is dealing with frequent governance controversies, which have raised concerns about its operational stability. Meanwhile, Chainlink, a leading player in the oracle space, is under pressure to find new growth avenues as it seeks to maintain its market dominance. Additionally, Pyth is making significant inroads into the prediction market, potentially altering the competitive landscape. The evolving dynamics in the oracle sector raise questions about which platform will emerge as indispensable in the future.

data.title: Eric Trump's Wealth Increases Amid American Bitcoin's Stock Decline
data.description: Eric Trump has reportedly increased his personal wealth from approximately $190 million to $280 million through American Bitcoin. According to Odaily, the company's stock has experienced a significant decline of about 92% from its peak, resulting in public investors losing around $500 million.

data.title: Eric Trump increased his personal wealth from approximately $190 million to $280 million through American Bitcoin.
data.description: Bitcoin News reported on the X platform that Eric Trump increased his personal wealth from approximately $190 million to $280 million through American Bitcoin. Simultaneously, the company's heavily diluted stock price fell by approximately 92% from its peak, resulting in a loss of approximately $500 million for public investors.

data.title: Apple Faces Extended Mac Mini and Mac Studio Shortages Amid Rising Demand
data.description: Apple CEO Tim Cook has indicated that shortages of Mac mini and Mac Studio devices may persist for several months. Some upgraded Mac mini orders are currently showing wait times of 16 to 18 weeks. According to NS3.AI, Mac revenue increased by 6% year over year, reaching $8.4 billion in the quarter. The rapid adoption of OpenClaw for local AI agents has driven developers to opt for high-memory Apple Silicon systems, contributing to the demand.

data.title: Pawn America Reaches $3.185 Million Settlement Over Data Breach
data.description: Pawn America has agreed to a $3.185 million class action settlement following a data breach that impacted over 530,000 customers in Minnesota and Wisconsin. According to NS3.AI, affected individuals can claim up to $5,000 for documented losses, while those without documentation are eligible for $30, and California residents can receive $50. The deadline for submitting claims is July 6th, with a final court hearing scheduled for September 9th.

data.title: Mediterranean Shipping Company to Launch New Service Connecting Europe and Middle East Ports
data.description: Mediterranean Shipping Company is set to introduce a new service linking Europe with isolated Middle Eastern ports through land transport across Saudi Arabia and small vessels within the Persian Gulf. According to Odaily, the service will commence on May 10 from Antwerp, with stops in Germany, Italy, Lithuania, Spain, Jeddah, and King Abdullah Port. From there, cargo will be transported by truck to Dammam. From Dammam, feeder vessels will connect to maritime gateways in Abu Dhabi, Dubai's Jebel Ali, as well as Bahrain, Iraq, and Kuwait.

data.title: Adam Back Faces Renewed Speculation About Bitcoin Invention
data.description: Adam Back has been repeatedly questioned over the past weeks about whether he is the inventor of Bitcoin. According to Bloomberg, these inquiries have come from various forums and individuals, reflecting ongoing speculation surrounding his potential role in the creation of the cryptocurrency.

data.title: Berkshire Hathaway's Cash Reserves Reach Record High Under New CEO
data.description: Berkshire Hathaway's cash reserves have surged to a record $397 billion in the first quarter under the leadership of new CEO Greg Abel. According to Odaily, this marks a significant increase from the slight dip in cash reserves at the end of last year, driven by net stock sales amounting to $8.1 billion during the period.

data.title: Berkshire Hathaway's cash reserves surged to a record $397 billion.
data.description: In Greg Abel's first quarter as CEO, Berkshire Hathaway's cash reserves jumped to a record high of $397 billion. While the company's cash reserves declined slightly at the end of last year, they increased significantly in the first quarter due to net sales of $8.1 billion in stock during that period. (Jinshi)

data.title: The world's largest container shipping company plans to bypass the Strait of Hormuz.
data.description: Mediterranean Shipping Company (MSC) plans to launch a new service connecting Europe with isolated Middle Eastern ports via land transport through Saudi Arabia and smaller vessels in the Persian Gulf. The service will depart from Antwerp on May 10, stopping at Germany, Italy, Lithuania, Spain, Jeddah, and King Abdullah before arriving in Dammam by truck. From Dammam, feeder vessels will connect to maritime gateways such as Abu Dhabi, Dubai's Jebel Ali, and Bahrain, Iraq, and Kuwait. (Jinshi)

data.title: Berkshire Hathaway Reports Q1 2026 Earnings
data.description: Berkshire Hathaway reported its first-quarter 2026 earnings, with revenue reaching $93.675 billion, compared to $89.725 billion in the same period last year. According to Jin10, the market had anticipated revenue of $89.274 billion. The company's net profit for the quarter was $10.106 billion, up from $4.603 billion in the previous year, although market expectations were higher at $11.762 billion.

data.title: Iran Open to Talks with U.S. but Rejects Coercion, Says Judiciary Chief
data.description: Iran's Judiciary Chief Ejei recently stated that Iran is open to negotiations with the United States but firmly opposes coercion. According to Odaily, Ejei emphasized that while discussions are reasonable and logical, they do not imply acceptance of U.S. demands or tolerance for threats. He further stressed that Iran does not seek war and hopes to avoid prolonged conflict.

data.title: Iran does not shy away from negotiations but opposes US coercion.
data.description: Iran's Chief Justice, Ajay Ejai, recently stated that Iran does not shy away from negotiations with the United States, but firmly opposes US coercion. He pointed out that negotiations are reasonable and logical, but this does not mean accepting the US's domineering demands, much less tolerating the US imposing its will through threats. At the same time, Ejai emphasized that Iran does not want war, and even less does it want a prolonged war. (CCTV International News)

data.title: Planet Evening News
data.description: 1. Iran says war with the US "may" resume; 2. Ethereum Foundation sells 10,000 ETH to BitMine OTC for the third time in two months; 3. AMD expands its Texas data center, Riot Platforms shares rise 8%; 4. A MEV bot exploits a Meteora pool vulnerability to turn $0.22 into $696,000; 5. Bitdeer produced 186 BTC last week and sold them all, leaving it with zero holdings; 6. Trump's approval ratings are low, Republicans rush to "redraw districts"; 7. Paradigm partners propose the PACTs mechanism to provide a protection path for quantum-fragile Bitcoin addresses; 8. LAB surges 200% intraday, currently trading at $2.07.

data.title: Riot Platforms Shares Rise 8% Following AMD Expansion in Texas
data.description: Riot Platforms' stock experienced an 8% increase after AMD expanded its facility in Rockdale, Texas. According to Odaily, this development underscores Riot's shift from Bitcoin mining to AI data centers.

data.title: Developer Claims Tool Can Recover $688M in Lost Bitcoin
data.description: A developer has claimed that a new CUDA-powered tool could recover 8,999 Bitcoin (BTC) lost in 2010 by a user known as "Stone Man," potentially reclaiming a fortune now worth approximately $688 million. According to BeInCrypto, the Bitcoin address has remained inactive for nearly 16 years, and a successful recovery would be among the largest ever recorded. The tool exploits weak entropy in early Bitcoin client keys, using GPU hardware to accelerate calculations. If successful, this method could impact the recovery of roughly 4 million BTC stranded in lost wallets.

data.title: AMD expands its Texas data center footprint; Riot Platforms shares rise 8%.
data.description: Bitcoin News reported on the X platform that Riot Platforms' stock price rose 8% after AMD expanded the capacity of its Rockdale, Texas site, highlighting Riot's transformation from Bitcoin mining to AI data centers.

data.title: Ethereum Foundation sold 10,000 ETH to BitMine OTC for the third time in two months.
data.description: The Ethereum Foundation sold 10,000 ETH to BitMine, marking its third over-the-counter transaction in two months. (Cointelegraph)

data.title: Riot's Q1 2026 Revenue Reaches $167.2 Million Amid Bitcoin Mining Challenges
data.description: Riot reported a revenue of $167.2 million for the first quarter of 2026, with $33.2 million generated from its newly established data center business. According to NS3.AI, the company's Bitcoin mining revenue decreased to $111.9 million, down from $142.9 million in the same period last year. This decline was attributed to lower average Bitcoin prices and a 24% increase in the global hash rate, which impacted the results. By the end of the quarter, Riot held 15,679 Bitcoin, valued at approximately $1.1 billion.

data.title: India Shifts Strategy on Trade Barriers to Attract Global Firms
data.description:
India is revising its approach to trade barriers, moving away from previous strategies aimed at attracting global firms to relocate production within its borders. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting that the initial plan, championed by Prime Minister Narendra Modi, focused on increasing trade barriers to incentivize international companies to establish manufacturing operations in India. However, this strategy is now being reconsidered as the country seeks alternative methods to draw foreign investment and boost its economic growth.

The shift comes amid changing global economic dynamics and the need for India to remain competitive in the international market. By reducing trade barriers, India aims to create a more favorable environment for foreign businesses, encouraging them to invest and expand their operations in the country. This move is expected to enhance India's position as a viable destination for global manufacturing and investment.

The decision to alter the trade barrier strategy reflects India's broader economic goals, which include increasing exports, creating jobs, and fostering sustainable growth. As India adapts to new economic realities, the government is exploring various avenues to attract foreign capital and strengthen its industrial base.

This strategic pivot is part of India's ongoing efforts to integrate more deeply into the global economy, leveraging its strengths and addressing challenges to ensure long-term prosperity.
